reference: Treatment for Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease includes short-acting bronchodilators such as a Salbutamol inhaler, long-acting bronchodilators, inhaled corticosteroids, and oxygen if there is respiratory failure. When a patient has an exacerbation, they may need systemic corticosteroids and even antibiotics if the reason for the exacerbation is a lung infection. The patient may also need non-invasive ventilation in the form of BiPAP or CPAP.
text: Treatment for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease include short-acting bronchodilators such as a salbutamol inhaler, long-acting bronchodilators, inhaled corticosteroids, and oxygen if there is respiratory failure. When a patient has an exacerbation, they may need systematic corticosteroids and even antibiotics if the reason for the exacerbation is a lung infection. The patient may also need non-invasive ventilation in the form of Fiapac or CPAC.
